title: Roasted Velvety Turkish Eggs Menemen
description: A hearty, comforting Turkish dish made with roasted whole wheat, velvety eggs, and spices. Perfect for a cozy breakfast or brunch.
instructions: Cut the whole wheat bread into small cubes and toast until lightly browned.
In a large skillet, heat the unsalted butter over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the red pepper flakes and cook for an additional 30 seconds.
Crack the eggs into a bowl and whisk together with a pinch of salt and pepper.
Pour the egg mixture over the garlic and pepper mixture in the skillet.
Stir the eggs constantly until they are cooked through, about 2-3 minutes.
Remove the skillet from the heat and stir in the toasted bread cubes, chopped parsley, and grated cheese.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake until the cheese is melted and bubbly, about 5-7 minutes.
Remove the skillet from the oven and let cool slightly before serving.
Serve the Roasted Velvety Turkish Eggs Menemen hot, garnished with additional parsley and cheese if desired.
This recipe is best served hot, straight from the oven.
Adjust the amount of red pepper flakes to your desired level of heat.
Add some diced ham or bacon for extra protein and flavor.
Experiment with different types of cheese for a unique twist on this recipe.
